title: Elementary
outcome: Move from survival English to connected everyday English for study, travel, work, and social life.

chapters.title: Solid Everyday Grammar
chapters.summary: Stabilize core grammar so common daily sentences feel predictable, accurate, and reusable.
lexicon: the usual way something happens every day
a common food eaten in many meals
an idea of what to do
a piece of work to complete
lessons: I, You, He, She, We, They
choose the right subject in a sentence
subject pronouns and be verb review
talk about finished actions in the past
Irregular Past Verbs in Real Speech
talk about future ideas and quick decisions
talk about things you can and cannot count
A, An, and The in Real Life
There Is, There Are, There Was, There Were
describe what exists now and before
there is and there are across time
describe actions and things more clearly
combine core grammar forms in one conversation

chapters.title: Experiences and Storytelling
chapters.summary: Move from isolated sentences to short stories about life, memories, and goals.
contexts: telling a friend about your life
lexicon: something you remember from the past
a trip from one place to another
a strong hope for the future
something that has happened to you
the act of deciding between options
lessons: switch between past and future in one topic
Talk About Places You Have Visited
talk about things you have done
add time meaning to experience sentences
talk about options and what you recommend
speak about the future more personally
connect memory, experience, and goals into one story

chapters.title: Travel, Shopping, and Services
chapters.summary: Build language needed for transport, booking, shopping, complaints, and service recovery.
lexicon: a reservation for travel or stay
a paper or digital proof of payment
the area where you wait for a train
the act of entering a plane or bus
the planned times for travel or work
extra waiting time before something starts
a fixed time to meet someone professionally
how big or small something is
the desk where service is given
lessons: ask about fit, color, and choice
fix time and schedule details clearly
At the Salon, Clinic, or Repair Shop
explain a practical need step by step
evaluate choices by cost, speed, and comfort

chapters.title: Study, Work, and Digital Life
chapters.summary: Gain language for study, office work, digital communication, and simple professional updates.
lexicon: the final time to finish something
to include a file with a message
a document showing your education and work
done from another place through technology
lessons: write and say short professional messages
repair confusion in study or work
share progress in a short format
Meetings: Join, Listen, and Speak Up
talk about urgency and order of work
describe what you can do well
can, have experience, and strength language
deliver a short end-to-end update at work

chapters.title: Relationships, Culture, and Community
chapters.summary: Speak about festivals, manners, community life, hobbies, wellness, and everyday opinions.
contexts: sharing opinions in a friendly circle
lexicon: a special celebration or holiday event
a nice thing you say about someone
what people should or should not do
the group of people living or working around you
good for your body or mind
an activity you do for enjoyment
polite care for people and rules
to do something special for an event
lessons: talk about cultural events and plans
talk about right and wrong behavior
share what you enjoy in free time
react to common topics around you
should, could, and why don't you
hold a longer social exchange with confidence
